summary.en: The city's polished heart, where historic brick facades house upscale restaurants, cocktail lounges, and independent shops.
A historic market district with red-brick streets, a bustling farmers market, and artisanal food shops, creating a relaxed, European-inspired atmosphere.
The historic and academic core of the University of Michigan, buzzing with student energy against a backdrop of stunning collegiate gothic architecture.
The social, beating heart of the university's undergraduate scene; a quintessential college strip with lively bars and casual eateries.
A slightly grittier, post-industrial area with a spirit of independence, home to legendary music venues and proper dive bars.
A relaxed, residential zone where locals and grad students enjoy innovative breweries and food spots away from the downtown crowds.

description.en: A dimly lit, speakeasy-style cocktail bar known for its expertly crafted drinks and robust food menu.
A swanky and intimate cocktail bar ideal for a date night, focusing on creative, high-quality cocktails.
A relaxed, whiskey-forward local's watering hole tucked away below Blue Tractor BBQ with a great beer list and zero pretense.
A universally loved Ann Arbor institution serving exceptional Neapolitan-style pizzas and handmade pastas in a lively atmosphere.
A vibrant, casual spot specializing in Cuban-inspired 'fritas' (spicy chorizo burgers) and 'batidos' (tropical milkshakes).
A magnificent 1928 movie palace with an opulent interior that hosts films, concerts, and events.
An iconic metaphysical bookstore with an upstairs tea room serving over 100 types of tea in a cozy, eclectic space.
A non-profit gallery and community space showcasing the creative pulse of the city through local and regional artists.
A beloved local quirk consisting of whimsical, miniature doors installed on local businesses at street level.
The coffee and pastry-focused sibling to the famous deli, perfect for a top-tier espresso and people-watching.
A world-class Jewish deli known for its massive, high-quality sandwiches and gourmet groceries; a quintessential Ann Arbor experience.
A premier food destination on Wednesdays and Saturdays, offering fresh produce and prepared foods from local vendors.
An indoor marketplace in a historic building, featuring independent vendors selling specialty foods, wine, gifts, and kitchenware.
A charming, tucked-away courtyard known for its historic homes and as a center for the local LGBTQ+ community.
An institution famous for its sprawling, tree-covered patio and sangria served in mason jars.
A city-wide favorite local roaster, perfect for fueling up with meticulously brewed coffee before exploring campus.
A no-frills, beloved campus sandwich shop famous for its Philly cheesesteaks.
A second, campus-adjacent location offering delicious Cuban-inspired street food to a student-heavy crowd.
Arguably the most beautiful part of campus, this enclosed quad features breathtaking collegiate gothic architecture.
The central heart of campus, a large green space that's a hub of student life, activism, and people-watching.

name.en: University of Michigan Museum of Art (UMMA)

description.en: A surprisingly comprehensive and free art museum with a collection spanning the globe and centuries.
An ever-changing canvas of street art that is a vibrant, chaotic, and uniquely Ann Arbor expression of public art.
An Ann Arbor staple since 1979, known for its massive outdoor patio and potent 'fishbowl' cocktails.
The quintessential divey college bar for upperclassmen, with sticky floors and loud music.
A slightly more modern take on the college bar, often serving as a starting point for a night out on South U.
A long-standing campus institution serving pizza, subs, and milkshakes with a nostalgic, old-school feel.
A massive boulder that serves as a public message board, painted over almost daily by student groups.
A stunning earthwork sculpture by Maya Lin on North Campus, featuring an undulating field of grass.
A beautifully restored Art Deco theater that is a cornerstone of Ann Arbor's independent film scene.
A legendary, no-frills live music venue where you can see local and national acts in an intimate space.
Widely considered Ann Arbor's best dive bar, this is a dark, raucous, cash-only billiards bar in a basement.
A quirky, multi-level bar that offers different vibes on each floor, perfect for a group that can't decide.
Ann Arbor's best ice cream shop, serving all-natural, homemade ice cream in creative, rotating flavors.
A classic, old-fashioned dairy and donut shop that has been serving the community for decades.
An interactive science museum for all ages, housed in a beautifully renovated historic fire station.
A historic commercial block from the 1860s with ornate brickwork and cast-iron details.
The alleys between Liberty and Washington often feature impressive murals and street art worth seeking out.
A fantastic indoor/outdoor space built from shipping containers, featuring a main bar, food trucks, and a coffee shop.
A specialty coffee roaster operating out of the HOMES Campus, serving meticulously prepared espresso drinks.
A small-batch brewery and artisan sausage maker with a cozy, rustic space and outdoor patio.
A permanent food vendor at the HOMES Campus serving delicious, wood-fired pizzas that pair perfectly with beer.
The largest stadium in the United States; a cathedral of college football whose scale is breathtaking even on non-game days.
A large, beautiful city park with walking paths, tennis courts, and playgrounds, perfect for a peaceful stroll.
A truly unique hidden gem that combines a microbrewery with an improv comedy theater.

prompt.en: Find the most interesting historic brick building and take a photo emphasizing its architectural details.
Capture a photo of a fancy cocktail or a cozy cup of tea that represents the sophisticated vibe of downtown.
Take a picture of a storefront window display from an independent shop that catches your eye.
As evening approaches, capture the warm glow of a streetlight on a downtown street.
Look for a small, unexpected piece of public art—a tiny sculpture, a sticker, or a mural—and snap a photo.
Find a puddle or a window and take a creative photo of the downtown buildings reflected in it.
Capture a scene that shows the mix of professionals, locals, and students that frequent the downtown area.
Find a doorway that looks like it leads to a secret or exclusive spot and take a mysterious photo.
Photograph a lively outdoor dining scene on a restaurant patio.
Find a cool neon sign and take a vibrant photo of it after dark.
Take a photo that captures the intellectual and literary feel of the area, perhaps featuring a bookstore or someone reading.
Stand on a street corner and take a picture looking straight up at the tops of the buildings against the sky.
Take an artful photo of a delicious-looking dish from one of the area's many restaurants.
During the day, find an interesting shadow cast by a building, a person, or a tree and capture it.
Get up close and take a photo of a unique texture you find, like old brick, a metal grate, or peeling paint.
Find the most colorful display of fresh produce or flowers and take a vibrant photo.
Photograph a beautifully crafted item from an artisanal shop, like a loaf of bread, a piece of cheese, or a handmade gift.
Take a low-angle shot that highlights the historic red-brick streets or sidewalks of Kerrytown.
Capture a candid moment of someone genuinely enjoying their food or drink in the neighborhood.
Find a scene that makes Kerrytown feel like a small, historic village and capture its essence.
Take a photo from a cafe patio that captures the flow of people and the relaxed atmosphere.
Find a sign with a classic or vintage font and take a picture of it.
Discover a quiet courtyard or tucked-away space and photograph its peaceful character.
Take a selfie with a famous or delicious-looking local food item.
Take a photo that tries to visually capture the sounds of the neighborhood, like a street musician or a bustling market.
Find a detail—like a window box, a narrow alley, or a cafe setting—that gives off a European vibe.
Photograph a product or sign that is proudly 'Michigan-made' or local to Ann Arbor.
Get a close-up, mouth-watering shot of a beautiful pastry or baked good from a local cafe.
Find a charming historic house in or near the district and capture its unique character.
Take a photo of a spot where people gather, like a market stall, a park bench, or a popular deli.
Capture the impressive details of the collegiate gothic architecture on a campus building.
Take a photo that encapsulates student energy: someone studying on the grass, rushing to class, or socializing.
Photograph the crisscrossing pathways of a central campus area, symbolizing different academic journeys.
Find an archway and use it to frame a shot of something interesting on the other side.
Take a picture of a cup of coffee or a snack that's fueling a study session.
Find a campus landmark or statue and take a creative selfie with it.
Capture a scene where art and academics intersect, like a sculpture near a library or a museum on campus.
Find a quiet bench or hidden corner perfect for reading and take a photo of it.
Find the university's colors, maize and blue, appearing naturally in a scene and photograph them.
Find a piece of street art or graffiti that makes a statement and capture it.
Take a photo that shows the contrast between historic and modern architecture in the area.
Capture a shot that highlights the beautiful, mature trees that are integrated into the campus landscape.
Photograph a classic, no-frills student meal from a campus-area eatery.
Take a photo of the exterior of a grand library, focusing on its lines and symmetry.
Subtly capture a detail of student fashion that you find interesting or unique.
Capture the young, loud, and fun energy of the South U strip in a single photo.
Take a picture of a bustling outdoor patio at a bar or restaurant.
Photograph a classic late-night college food like pizza or a sub.
Take a selfie that includes a lively group of people in the background.
Find a landmark or piece of public art that is clearly part of the student culture and photograph it.
Find the most interesting or classic-looking bar sign and snap a picture.
Take a photo of a unique or colorful drink that represents the social scene here.
Find a building with Art Deco architecture and capture a close-up of its stylish details.
Photograph a spot that is clearly a popular hangout for students.
Capture the blur of lights and motion on the street at night.
Find someone showing their school spirit by wearing university colors and snap a photo.
Get a mouth-watering close-up shot of a slice of pizza.
Find a place where old and new student life collide, like a historic building with modern posters, and photograph it.
Capture the dynamic of a group of friends walking and talking on the street.
Even in a busy area, find a small moment or place of calm and capture it.
Take a photo that captures the gritty, independent, rock-and-roll spirit of the West Side.
Find a scene that perfectly represents a classic dive bar: a neon sign, a pool table, or a dimly lit corner.
Photograph a building or detail that hints at the area's post-industrial history.
Explore an alley and find the most compelling piece of street art or mural.
Take an amazing photo of a classic sweet treat, like an ice cream cone or a donut.
Capture the exterior of a music venue in a way that makes you feel the energy inside.
Find an understated or hidden doorway to a bar or venue and take a photo that makes it look intriguing.
Photograph something that feels like a trip back in time, like a vintage sign or an old-fashioned shop.
Take a photo of a neon sign reflected in a puddle or window after dark.
Capture a scene that feels authentically local and separate from the university's influence.
Take a close-up photo focusing on the texture of an old brick wall in the neighborhood.
Play with the contrast between light and shadow in a narrow alley or on a building facade.
During the day, find a quiet spot in this typically noisy neighborhood and capture the stillness.
Take a selfie that shows off your independent spirit, with a cool mural or gritty backdrop.
Find a building with an old fire escape and take a photo focusing on its geometric shapes and lines.
Capture a scene that feels like a genuine local hangout, away from the main tourist and student crowds.
Take an artistic photo of a craft beer or a house-made sausage.
Find an example of industrial-chic design, like a building made of shipping containers or a renovated warehouse, and photograph it.
Find a tranquil spot in a park and take a photo that conveys a sense of peace and quiet.
Take a photo that captures the immense, breathtaking scale of a giant sports stadium.
Photograph a quiet, tree-lined residential street in the neighborhood.
Find a unique, slightly hidden spot that feels like a local secret and capture its charm.
Get a close-up shot of a beautifully made latte or specialty coffee drink.
Find a cool-looking food truck and take a picture of its menu or design.
Photograph a space where the local community gathers to eat, drink, and socialize.
Take a selfie that shows you enjoying the relaxed, laid-back atmosphere of the neighborhood.
Find a place that combines two unexpected things, like a brewery and a theater, and capture its unique character.
Photograph a welcoming beer garden or outdoor patio space.
Find a house or a garden that has a lot of unique personality and take a photo.
Take a photo down a side street or path that looks less traveled.
